A senior trader, Ousmane Bizimana, circulated fabricated screenshots of order books to outside contacts as a joke; the images went viral and were widely read as evidence that prices on a Canadian marketplace were being manipulated, even though no client of the firm was harmed and no actual trading was affected. An internal review also finds that the episode was possible because the firm had never trained the trading floor on external-communication ethics, and executives propose handling the entire matter quietly with no staff communication. The firm now assesses the conduct and its institutional response under CIRO standards and the syllabus's ethics framework.
questions.stem: The internal review attributes the episode partly to the firm never having educated the trading floor on external-communication ethics, and the executives' quiet-handling proposal would keep staff uninformed even now. Mapped to the syllabus's outcome 1.18 pillars, the firm's existing gap and the proposal together implicate which pillars?

options.A: Only dealing with compensation conflicts, since traders are paid on performance
options.B: Training and awareness (the never-trained floor) and transparency (the proposal to conceal the episode from staff)
options.C: Neither — outcome 1.18 pillars apply only to boards of public companies
options.D: Only oversight and accountability, because an executive proposed the quiet handling

questions.rationale: Outcome 1.18's pillars are transparency, dealing with compensation conflicts, oversight and accountability, and training and awareness. A trading floor never educated on the relevant ethics is a training-and-awareness gap, and deliberately concealing the episode and its lessons from staff offends transparency. Option A reaches for compensation conflicts on facts that show none; option D names a real pillar but captures only who made the proposal, not the two failures identified; option C invents a public-company limitation absent from the parameter.
questions.source_quote: "• Training and awareness" — syllabus-raw.txt ~line 396
questions.qa_note: Gate 2 wave 8: single-bullet span (24 chars, clears the 20-char threshold); "Transparency" (keyed jointly) verifies verbatim at ~384; 1.18 bullets are interleaved with 1.22 bullets by two-column extraction so no longer clean span exists.

A senior trader, Ousmane Bizimana, circulated fabricated screenshots of order books to outside contacts as a joke; the images went viral and were widely read as evidence that prices on a Canadian marketplace were being manipulated, even though no client of the firm was harmed and no actual trading was affected. An internal review also finds that the episode was possible because the firm had never trained the trading floor on external-communication ethics, and executives propose handling the entire matter quietly with no staff communication. The firm now assesses the conduct and its institutional response under CIRO standards and the syllabus's ethics framework.
questions.stem: Within weeks of the screenshots going viral, the regulator opens inquiries into the firm's communication controls, national media coverage damages the firm's public standing, and several embarrassed employees begin job-hunting. Mapping these three developments to the syllabus's outcome 1.19 list, they correspond respectively to:

options.A: Regulatory scrutiny; reputational damage; and employee morale and turnover
options.B: Legal consequences; client confidence and turnover; and transparency
options.C: Pooling of client assets; regulatory scrutiny; and training and awareness
options.D: Reputational damage; legal consequences; and oversight and accountability

questions.rationale: The outcome 1.19 consequences are legal consequences, reputational damage, regulatory scrutiny, employee morale and turnover, and client confidence and turnover. The regulator's inquiries are regulatory scrutiny; damaged public standing is reputational damage; and embarrassed staff job-hunting is employee morale and turnover. Option B miscodes the inquiries as legal consequences (no lawsuit or charge yet exists) and imports transparency from the 1.18 pillars; options C and D mix in CIPF's pooling role and 1.18 governance pillars, neither of which is a 1.19 consequence.
questions.source_quote: "• Legal consequences • Reputational damage • Regulatory scrutiny" — syllabus-raw.txt ~line 404

An internal review also finds that the episode was possible because the firm had never trained the trading floor on external-communication ethics, and executives propose handling the entire matter quietly with no staff communication. The firm now assesses the conduct and its institutional response under CIRO standards and the syllabus's ethics framework.
questions.stem: Remediating, Lestock Bridge adopts a strict internal code on external communications that goes well beyond what any rule or client agreement requires, and binds all staff to it. In the outcome 1.12 classification of the Investment Dealer's obligations, the duties created by this internal code are:

options.A: Legislative, because the code responds to a regulatory episode
options.B: Contractual, because employees are paid to follow it
options.C: Inapplicable, because outcome 1.12 covers only duties owed to clients with open accounts
options.D: Organizational — obligations arising from the firm's own internal framework, the third source alongside legislative and contractual obligations

questions.rationale: Outcome 1.12 classifies the Investment Dealer's obligations to clients and others into legislative, contractual, and organizational sources; a self-imposed internal code stricter than any rule or agreement is the organizational source — the firm's own framework. Option A confuses what prompted the code with its legal source: it is not legislation. Option B stretches the employment relationship to relabel an internal standard as a client-facing contractual obligation. Option C truncates the outcome, which expressly covers obligations 'to clients and others.'
questions.source_quote: "Understand the Investment Dealer's obligations to clients and others. Consider: • Legislative" — syllabus-raw.txt ~line 357
